table_summary: Detailed records of placement balances, including account, customer, product, and financial information for various placements, with amounts in multiple currencies and key dates for each record.

column_descriptions.description: Text field describing the module or business process context for the placement record. Used for categorization or grouping.
Text code uniquely identifying the account associated with the placement. Used for linking to account-level details.
Text field containing the name or label of the account. Provides a descriptive identifier for the account.
Text code uniquely identifying the customer involved in the placement. Used for customer-level tracking and reporting.
Text field containing the full name of the customer. Used for display and identification purposes.

Three-character code indicating the currency of all monetary values in this row (e.g., 'MNT' for Mongolian Tugrik, 'USD' for US Dollar, 'JPY' for Japanese Yen, 'CNY' for Chinese Yuan, 'EUR' for Euro). All numeric amount columns (such as fine_int_rcv, int_rcv, paid_princ, principal, etc.) are denominated in this currency.
Numeric value representing the amount of received fine interest for the placement. The currency is determined by the cur_code column.
Numeric value indicating the interest rate applied to the placement, typically as a percentage per annum.
Numeric value representing the amount of interest received for the placement. The currency is determined by the cur_code column.
Numeric value representing the billed interest received for the placement. The currency is determined by the cur_code column.
Date field indicating the maturity date of the placement, i.e., when the principal is due to be repaid.
Text field (possibly a code or reference) related to paid interest. The exact meaning may depend on business context; could be a transaction or voucher code.
Text field (possibly a code or reference) related to paid principal. The exact meaning may depend on business context; could be a transaction or voucher code.
Numeric value representing the principal amount of the placement. The currency is determined by the cur_code column.
Text code uniquely identifying the product associated with the placement. Used for product-level analysis.
Text field containing the name of the product associated with the placement.
Text field describing the type or category of the placement or product.
Numeric value, possibly representing a fee, commission, or another rate relevant to the placement. The exact business meaning may depend on context.
Numeric value representing a rating or score associated with the placement, customer, or product. Used for risk or quality assessment.
Numeric value representing the amount of security or collateral associated with the placement. The currency is determined by the cur_code column.
Numeric value indicating the term length of the placement, likely in days.
Date field indicating the transaction date for the placement record. Essential for filtering and time-based analysis.
Date field indicating the value date for the placement, i.e., when the funds become effective or are settled.
Text field describing the current status or remarks for the placement record. Used for tracking progress, issues, or notes.
